content.full_text:
WILBERT W. SMALLWOOD
EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR

Administrative Office
P.O. Box 1176 • Sonora, CA 95370

Mother Lode
Job Training Agency

(209) 533-5670

AMADOR COUNTY • CALAVERAS COUNTY • MARIPOSA COUNTY • TUOLUMNE COUNTY

February 23, 1990


Mr. Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


Dear Mr. Rothman:

As we previously discussed, I am still seeking clarification regarding the
disclosure requirements which apply to our agency's Finance Director. This
matter needs immediate attention because the filing deadline is March 8.

Please be advised that the responsibilities of this position involve
preliminary approval of claims for payment submitted by vendors and staff to
the agency. It also involves processing of journal entries, preparation of
budgets, development and implementation of fiscal procedures, reconciliation
of accounts, and numerous other duties as specified on the attached job
description. However, the Finance Director does not have authority to give
final approval to any of the aforementioned.

Furthermore, and as I mentioned before, the Finance Director: (1) is not a
"public official", (2) does not "manage the investment of public funds", and
(3) does not hold an elective position. It was my understanding from our
telephone discussion of 1/17/90 that because neither of these circumstances
exists, the Finance Director is not in fact required to file Form 721 as
stated in the 1/9/90 letter from Kathryn Donovan. However, the position was
also deleted by the FPPC from the list of designated employees in our approved
Conflict of Interest Code.

My questions are:

1.     What are the Statement of Economic Interest (or other) filing
       obligations of our agency's Finance Director?

2.     If Form 730 is required, is it necessary to obtain FPPC approval of a
       revised Conflict of Interest Code which reinstates the Finance Director
       on the list of designated employees? If so, what, if anything, do you
       need from this agency in order to obtain such approval?

3.     If Form 721 is required, please send me a copy as soon as possible.

Page -2-
Jonathan S. Rothman
February 23, 1990


I hope the information I've provided here is sufficient for you to render an
official determination; but please call if you need anything further.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Susan M. Lemke
Program Director

/grm
attachment
cc: Barbara S. Cunningham
    Wilbert W. Smallwood


ROTHMN2.23/SL #2 89/90

Mother Lode Job Training Agency
July 1989

FINANCE DIRECTOR

DEFINITION:

Under general direction, performs responsible duties related to the fiscal
planning and management of employment and training program under the Job
Training Partnership Act (JTPA) and Greater Avenues for Independence (GAIN) in
Amador, Calaveras, Mariposa and Tuolumne Counties.

EXAMPLES OF DUTIES:

Establishes and implements procedures for all fiscal reporting necessary for
the operation of JTPA and GAIN programs; develops and makes recommendations
for fiscal policies and procedures; prepares budgets, forecasts and cost
allocation plans; develops and implements automated financial systems;
prepares monthly, quarterly, annual and closeout reports; performs internal
monitoring of budget versus actual expenditures; monitors subcontractors for
compliance with fiscal requirements; supervises Fiscal Assistant; ensures the
timeliness and accuracy of all required reports, invoices, cash requests,
cost reviews and gives preliminary approval to all claims, invoices, purchase
orders, requisitions, contracts, etc.; reconciles agency accounts to county
reports; coordinates fiscal activities between Agency and County of Tuolumne;
works in conjunction with Program Coordinators in developing county cost
allocation plans and budgets; provides technical assistance and training to
staff and subcontractors; coordinates inside and outside audits; resolves
financial findings of monitoring audits; interprets applicable financial
laws and regulations, and implements policies and procedures to ensure
compliance; establishes and implements procedures for property management,
including inventory and purchasing systems; prepares budgets and fiscal
planning information for the job training plan and MIJTA contracts; places,
reviews and updates agency insurance coverage; implements procedures to ensure
the accurate payrolling of employees; meets and confers with Executive
Director and Agency staff on a continual basis; attends meetings, training
seminars and conferences as needed to maintain effectiveness; provides other
administrative support as assigned.

DESIRABLE QUALIFICATIONS:

Ability to:

- Interpret and implement applicable laws, regulations, policies and
  procedures;
- Plan, develop and manage a complex system of fiscal recordkeeping and
  reporting;
- Prepare accurate and concise reports;
- Perform under pressure with minimal supervision;
- Establish and maintain effective working relationships with a wide variety
  of individuals, groups, and organizations.

Mother Lode Job Training Agency                                July 1989

                              FINANCE DIRECTOR

Knowledge of:

-  Generally accepted accounting principles;
-  Laws, regulations, policies and procedures pertaining to the
   administration of JTPA and GRAIN programs;
-  Standard accounting and bookkeeping methods;
-  Principles of governmental and/or fund accounting;
-  Fiscal monitoring procedures and techniques;
-  Automated financial systems.


Education and Experience:

-  A Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Business Administration, or a related
   field; and
-  A minimum of two years of responsible and progressive employment
   experience in accounting, business administration, or a related field,
   including one year in a lead capacity.

OR

-  Any equivalent combination of experience and training which has provided
   the above listed knowledge and abilities.

License:

A valid California operator's license issued by the State Department of Motor
Vehicles.   (Individual may be required to use his/her personal vehicle to
perform out-of-office visits, and would be reimbursed for such use at the
prevailing rate.)

WILBERT W. SMALLWOOD
EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR

Administrative Office
P.O. Box 1176 • Sonora, CA 95370

(209) 533-5670

Mother Lode
Job Training Agency

AMADOR COUNTY • CALAVERAS COUNTY • MARIPOSA COUNTY • TUOLUMNE COUNTY


January 17, 1990


Mr. John Rothman
Legal Division
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


Dear Mr. Rothman:

Based on our telephone conversation this afternoon, it is my understanding
that because our agency's Finance Director does not "manage the investment of
public funds" and is not an elected official, she is not required to submit
Form 721 at this time. I will be expecting to receive a copy of implementing
regulations which will eventually clarify the categories of public
employees/officials who are subject to the Section 87200 requirements.

It is also my understanding that our agency's Finance Director is not subject
to conflict of interest disclosure requirements, and therefore will remain
excluded from the list of designated employees.

Please send me confirmation that the foregoing is accurate.

Thanks for all your help.

Sincerely,

Susan M. Lemke
Program Director

/grm

cc: Barbara Cunningham
Wilbert W. Smallwood

Thanks for all your help.

Sincerely,

Susan M. Leake
Program Director

/grm

cc: Barbara Cunningham
Wilbert W. Smallwood
